info.name: SDG Recycling Sandbox API
info.description:
Beginner-friendly Postman collection for testing the current backend during development.

Current live endpoints:
- GET /
- GET /api/v1/health
- GET /api/v1/db-test
- POST /api/v1/uploads/mission-proof

Planned system enum reference:
- User roles: USER, ADMIN
- Mission status: DRAFT, ACTIVE, ARCHIVED
- Mission type: QUANTITY, TIME, STREAK, EVENT, QUIZ
- Approval mode: AUTO, MANUAL
- Submission status: PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, DUPLICATE
- Content status: DRAFT, PUBLISHED, ARCHIVED
- Quiz attempt status: PASSED, FAILED, IN_PROGRESS
- Badge tiers: BRONZE, SILVER, GOLD
- Badge criteria types: MISSIONS_COMPLETED, QUIZ_PASS, LEARNING_STREAK, POINTS_EARNED
- Points event types: MISSION_APPROVED, QUIZ_PASSED, BADGE_AWARDED
- Upload purpose: MISSION_PROOF, CONTENT_IMAGE, PROFILE_IMAGE

Note: most of the planned enum values above are not implemented in the current backend yet. They are included here so the Postman file stays easy to grow as the API expands.

item.name: Smoke Checks
item.description: Basic requests to confirm the backend is running before testing deeper features.

request.description:
Use this first to confirm the backend server is reachable.

Success looks like:
- HTTP 200
- JSON response with a `message`

Setup:
- Start the backend locally with `npm run dev` or `npm start`.

Checks the API health endpoint.

Success looks like:
- HTTP 200
- `status` equals `ok`
- `message` confirms the sandbox backend is running

Setup:
- Backend server must be running.

exec: pm.test("Health response matches expected shape", function () {

item.name: Database
item.description: Checks that Prisma can reach the configured PostgreSQL database.

request.description:
Verifies the backend can connect to the database.

Success looks like:
- HTTP 200
- `status` equals `ok`
- `currentTime` is returned from the database query

Setup:
- Backend server must be running
- `DATABASE_URL` must point to a working PostgreSQL database

exec: pm.test("Database response matches expected shape", function () {

item.name: Uploads
item.description: Tests file upload behavior, including the success path and a simple validation error case.

request.description:
Uploads one mission proof file to Azure Blob Storage and stores metadata in the database.

Success looks like:
- HTTP 201
- `message` confirms the upload worked
- `blobName` and `url` are returned

Setup:
- Backend server must be running
- `DATABASE_URL` must be valid
- `AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING` must be configured
- In form-data, the file field name must be exactly `file`
- Planned upload purpose enum for future APIs: `{{uploadPurposeMissionProof}}`

formdata.description: Choose any sample file from your computer when running this request in Postman.

exec: pm.test("Upload response matches expected shape", function () {

request.description:
Checks the beginner-friendly validation message when no file is sent.

Success looks like:
- HTTP 400
- Response message explains that the `file` field is required

Setup:
- Backend server must be running

exec: pm.test("Missing file message is clear", function () {
pm.expect(data.message).to.eql("A file is required under the field name 'file'.");

item.name: Future APIs
item.description: Planned API areas based on the backend data model you described. These folders act as a roadmap for future Postman requests even though the routes are not implemented yet.

item.name: Users
item.description:
Planned user fields: id, name, email, passwordHash, role, createdAt, updatedAt.

Important enums:
- role: `{{userRoleUser}}`, `{{userRoleAdmin}}`

item.name: Missions
item.description:
Planned mission fields: id, title, description, missionType, points, startDate, endDate, submissionCap, approvalMode, status, createdById, createdAt, updatedAt.

Important enums:
- missionType: `{{missionTypeQuantity}}`, `{{missionTypeTime}}`, `{{missionTypeStreak}}`, `{{missionTypeEvent}}`, `{{missionTypeQuiz}}`
- approvalMode: `{{approvalModeAuto}}`, `{{approvalModeManual}}`
- status: `{{missionStatusDraft}}`, `{{missionStatusActive}}`, `{{missionStatusArchived}}`

Suggested future collection variables already included:
- `missionId`
- `adminUserId`

item.name: Submissions
item.description:
Planned submission fields: id, missionId, userId, proofText, proofImageUrl, proofImageBlobName, quantity, status, reviewNote, reviewedById, submittedAt, reviewedAt.

Important enums:
- status: `{{submissionStatusPending}}`, `{{submissionStatusApproved}}`, `{{submissionStatusRejected}}`, `{{submissionStatusDuplicate}}`
- `DUPLICATE` is expected to map to HTTP 409 Conflict in future APIs.

Suggested future collection variables already included:
- `submissionId`
- `reviewedById`

item.name: Points
item.description:
Planned points event fields: id, userId, missionId, submissionId, eventType, points, status, emittedAt.

Important enums:
- eventType: `{{pointsEventMissionApproved}}`, `{{pointsEventQuizPassed}}`, `{{pointsEventBadgeAwarded}}`

item.name: Content
item.description:
Planned content fields: id, title, body, category, tags, version, status, createdById, createdAt, updatedAt.

Important enums:
- status: `{{contentStatusDraft}}`, `{{contentStatusPublished}}`, `{{contentStatusArchived}}`

item.name: Quizzes
item.description:
Planned quiz-related tables include quiz, quiz questions, and quiz attempts.

Quiz fields: id, contentId, title, passingScore, status, createdAt, updatedAt.
Quiz question fields: id, quizId, questionText, options, correctAnswer, points.
Quiz attempt fields: id, quizId, userId, score, totalQuestions, correctAnswers, passed, answers, attemptedAt.

Important enums:
- quiz attempt status: `{{quizAttemptPassed}}`, `{{quizAttemptFailed}}`, `{{quizAttemptInProgress}}`
- content or quiz publication status can reuse `{{contentStatusPublished}}`

item.name: Badges
item.description:
Planned badge fields: id, name, description, tier, criteriaType, criteriaValue, status, createdAt.

Important enums:
- tier: `{{badgeTierBronze}}`, `{{badgeTierSilver}}`, `{{badgeTierGold}}`
- criteriaType: `{{badgeCriteriaMissionsCompleted}}`, `{{badgeCriteriaQuizPass}}`, `{{badgeCriteriaLearningStreak}}`, `{{badgeCriteriaPointsEarned}}`

item.name: Uploads Reference
item.description:
Planned upload table fields: id, userId, containerName, blobName, fileUrl, mimeType, fileSize, purpose, createdAt.

Important enums:
- purpose: `{{uploadPurposeMissionProof}}`, `{{uploadPurposeContentImage}}`, `{{uploadPurposeProfileImage}}`
